docs.title: Field Validation
docs.text: Field Validation Rules id Alphanumeric, underscores, dashes only. Max 64 chars. branch Must reference an existing branch ID x, y Numeric coordinates (auto-converted from gridX/gridY with 140px spacing) maxRank Integer ≥ 1 cost Array length must match maxRank, all values ≥ 0 requiredLevel Array length must match maxRank, all values ≥ 0 effectValue Array length must match maxRank (optional) requires Must reference existing node IDs effectType Must be a valid registered effect type callback Alphanumeric only (no special characters or spaces)

docs.title: Option 1: Manual JSON
docs.text:
Create a file in data/skillforge/trees/ on your server:

// data/skillforge/trees/my_custom_tree.json

Then reload:

skilltree_reload_trees

docs.title: Option 2: Using the Visual Editor
docs.text:
Open the editor:

skilltree_editor

Use the HTML interface to create, edit, and save trees.

docs.title: Option 3: Programmatically
docs.text:
local treeData = {
    id = "my_custom_tree",
    name = "My Custom Tree",
    description = "Created via script",
    author = "Me",
    version = "1.0.0",
    branches = { ... },
    nodes = { ... }
}
SkillTree.SaveTree("my_custom_tree", treeData)

docs.title: Template File
docs.text: A ready-to-use template is available at: lua/skillforge/default_trees/_TEMPLATE.json

docs.title: Tree Validation
docs.text: Every tree is automatically validated when loaded, saved, or deployed. The validator ensures structural integrity and security.

docs.title: Structural Checks
docs.text: Check Error Message Details Root type Tree data must be a table Root must be a table ID Tree must have a valid ID string Must be non-empty string ID chars Tree ID contains unsafe characters Only alphanumeric, -, _ Name Tree must have a name Must be non-empty string Branches Tree must have a branches table Must exist and be a table Nodes Tree must have a nodes table Must exist and be a table

docs.title: Branch Validation
docs.text: Check Error Message Branch ID Branch ID is invalid (alphanumeric + - + _) Branch type Branch must be a table Branch name Branch is missing a name

docs.title: Node Validation
docs.text: Check Error Message Node ID Node ID is invalid Node type Node must be a table Node name Node is missing a name Branch reference Node references non-existent branch Position Node requires valid numeric X and Y coordinates Max rank Node must have a maxRank integer > 0 Cost array Node requires a 'cost' array matching its maxRank Cost values Node has invalid cost value at rank Required level Node requires a 'requiredLevel' array Effect values Node has invalid 'effectValue' array length Prerequisites Node requires non-existent node Effect type Node has invalid effectType Callback Node has invalid callback identifier name

docs.title: Security Scanning
docs.text:
The validator scans for suspicious content to prevent code injection through tree JSON files:

lua/skillforge/sv_tree_validator.lua
local function scanForCode(tbl)
    for k, v in pairs(tbl) do
        if type(v) == "string" then
            local lowerStr = string.lower(v)
            if string.find(lowerStr, "function%s*%(")
               or string.find(lowerStr, "ply:")
               or string.find(lowerStr, "os%.")
               or string.find(lowerStr, "http%.")
               or string.find(lowerStr, "runstring")
               or string.find(lowerStr, "debug%.") then
                return false, "Suspicious string containing executable code"
            end
        end
    end
end

docs.title: Cycle Detection
docs.text:
The validator runs a DFS-based cycle detection on the prerequisite graph:

Input:  A → B → C → A
Output: "Prerequisite cycle detected involving node 'A'"

docs.title: Via console command
docs.text:
skilltree_validate_trees

This validates all loaded trees and reports results.

docs.title: Via API
docs.text:
local isValid, err = SkillTree.ValidateTree(treeData)
if not isValid then
    print("Tree validation failed: " .. err)
end

docs.title: Automatic validation
docs.text: Validation runs automatically when: - A tree is loaded from disk - A tree is saved via the editor - A tree is imported via JSON - Default trees are deployed

docs.title: Visual Editor
docs.text: Skillforge includes a full HTML/DHTML visual editor for creating and modifying skill trees without writing JSON by hand.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The visual editor allows server administrators to:

  • Create new skill trees
  • Add and configure branches
  • Add and configure nodes
  • Create prerequisite connections between nodes
  • Position nodes on a grid
  • Preview the tree layout
  • Save directly to the server

docs.title: Accessing the Editor
docs.text:
Open the editor with the console command:

skilltree_editor

Requires the skilltree.admin.editor permission.

docs.title: Editor Features
docs.text: Feature Description Canvas Zoomable, panable canvas for tree layout Node Inspector Click any node to edit its properties Branch Manager Create, edit, and delete branches Connection Tool Draw connections between nodes Grid Snapping Auto-snap nodes to grid positions Real-time Preview See the tree as players will see it Validation Automatic validation on save

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Getting Started — First steps with the editor
  • Creating Trees — Create a tree from scratch
  • Editing Nodes — Add, edit, and delete nodes
  • Branches & Connections — Work with branches and prerequisite links
  • Export & Import — Import/export tree JSON

docs.title: Add Branch
docs.text:
1. Click Add Branch in the toolbar
  2. Fill in:
  3. ID: Unique identifier
  4. Name: Display name
  5. Description: Branch description
  6. Color: Hex color for branch nodes
  7. Icon: Choose from available icons

docs.title: Edit Branch
docs.text:
1. Click a branch in the branch list
  2. Edit properties in the inspector

docs.title: Delete Branch
docs.text:
1. Select branch in the branch list
  2. Click delete
  3. All nodes in this branch must be reassigned or deleted

docs.title: Connections (Prerequisites)
docs.text: Connections create the prerequisite relationships between nodes.

docs.title: Create a Connection
docs.text:
1. Enable the Connection Tool (shortcut: C)
  2. Click on the source node (prerequisite)
  3. Click on the target node (requires the source)
  4. A line appears connecting them

docs.title: Visual Indicators
docs.text:
• Arrow direction: Source → Target
  • Color: Matches the branch color
  • Line style: Solid for available, dashed if locked

docs.title: Editing Connections
docs.text:
• Click a connection line to select it
  • Press Delete to remove the connection
  • Drag connection points to reconnect

docs.title: Connection Validation
docs.text:
The editor validates connections in real-time:

  • ✅ Connection valid
  • ⚠️ Creates a circular dependency (blocked)
  • ❌ Target node references non-existent source

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Keep branches to 3-4 nodes deep max
  • Use consistent color coding per branch
  • Place root nodes (no prerequisites) at the top
  • Chain prerequisites logically: basic → advanced → mastery
  • Use connection lines to create clear progression paths

docs.title: New Tree From Scratch
docs.text:
1. Click File → New Tree
  2. Set the tree properties:
  3. ID: my_custom_tree (unique, alphanumeric + underscores)
  4. Name: "My Custom Tree"
  5. Description: Brief description
  6. Author: Your name
  7. Version: 1.0.0

docs.title: Adding Branches
docs.text:
1. Click Add Branch
  2. Set properties:
  3. ID: combat (unique per tree)
  4. Name: "Combat"
  5. Description: "Combat skills"
  6. Color: #ef4444 (hex color)
  7. Icon: Select from available icons

docs.title: Adding Nodes
docs.text:
1. Right-click on the canvas
  2. Select Add Node
  3. Click to place it on the grid
  4. Configure in the inspector panel

docs.title: Required Node Properties
docs.text: Property Description ID Unique node identifier Name Display name Branch Which branch it belongs to Max Rank Max upgrade level (1-5) Cost Skill point cost per rank Required Level Level requirement per rank

docs.title: Optional Node Properties
docs.text: Property Description Effect Type Type of effect this node grants Effect Value Value per rank Prerequisites Other nodes required first Callback Developer callback name Active Ability Enable cooldown/duration for active skills

docs.title: Saving
docs.text: Click Save to write the tree to the server. The tree is validated before saving. If validation fails, error messages will indicate what needs to be fixed.

docs.title: Selecting a Node
docs.text: Click on any node on the canvas. The inspector panel on the right will show its properties.

docs.title: General
docs.text: Field Description ID Unique identifier (read-only after creation) Name Display name shown to players Description Tooltip description Branch Branch this node belongs to

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
Field Description Grid X Column position (0 = leftmost) Grid Y Row position (0 = top)

Drag the node to reposition, or enter coordinates manually. Grid spacing is 140px.

docs.title: Ranks
docs.text: Field Description Max Rank How many times this skill can be upgraded Cost 1-5 Skill point cost for each rank Level Req 1-5 Required player level for each rank

docs.title: Effects
docs.text: Field Description Effect Type Choose from 28 effect types Effect Value 1-5 Value for each rank

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: The requires field lists other node IDs that must be unlocked first. Use the connection tool to visually create prerequisites.

docs.title: Active Ability
docs.text:
Toggle to make this an active ability, then set:

Field Description Cooldown Seconds between uses Duration Duration of the effect (if applicable)

docs.title: Deleting a Node
docs.text:
1. Select the node
  2. Press Delete key or click the delete button in the inspector
  3. Confirm deletion

Connection removal

Deleting a node also removes all its connections. Other nodes that required this node will become invalid until reconnected.

docs.title: From the Editor
docs.text:
1. Open the tree you want to export
  2. Click File → Export
  3. The JSON is copied to your clipboard
  4. Paste it into a file and save

docs.title: From the Server
docs.text:
local json = SkillTree.ExportTree("police_tree")
file.Write("my_exported_tree.json", json)

docs.title: Via Console Command
docs.text:
1. Have your JSON ready
  2. Open the editor
  3. Click File → Import
  4. Paste the JSON
  5. The tree appears on the canvas
  6. Review and save

docs.title: Via API
docs.text:
local json = file.Read("my_custom_tree.json", "DATA")
local ok, treeID = SkillTree.ImportTree(json)
if ok then
    print("Tree imported: " .. treeID)
else
    print("Import failed: " .. treeID)  -- Contains error message
end

docs.title: Via File Copy
docs.text:
Copy the JSON file to data/skillforge/trees/ on your server:

data/skillforge/trees/my_custom_tree.json

Then reload:

skilltree_reload_trees

docs.title: Import Validation
docs.text:
When importing, the tree is automatically validated. Common errors:

Error Cause Invalid JSON format Syntax error in the JSON Missing tree 'id' No id field in root Validation failed Tree structure doesn't meet requirements

docs.title: Sharing Trees
docs.text:
You can share tree JSON files with other server owners:

{
    "id": "shared_tree",
    "name": "Shared Community Tree",
    "author": "Community Member",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    ...
}

To install: 1. Copy the JSON file 2. Import via editor or file copy 3. Map it to a job in your config

docs.title: Opening the Editor
docs.text:
skilltree_editor

Requires: skilltree.admin.editor permission.

docs.title: Interface Overview
docs.text:
The editor interface consists of:

  1. Toolbar (top) — Tools for creating branches, nodes, and connections
  2. Canvas (center) — Drag-and-drop workspace for tree layout
  3. Inspector (right panel) — Properties panel for selected elements
  4. Branch List (left panel) — List of all branches in the current tree

docs.title: Basic Navigation
docs.text: Action Mouse/Keyboard Pan Click and drag on empty canvas space Zoom Mouse scroll wheel Select node Left click on a node Deselect Click on empty canvas Delete selected Delete key

docs.title: Quick Workflow
docs.text:
1. Open the editor — skilltree_editor
  2. Create a branch — Click "Add Branch" and set name/color
  3. Add nodes — Right-click on the canvas and select "Add Node"
  4. Position nodes — Drag nodes to desired grid positions
  5. Connect nodes — Drag from a node's output to another's input
  6. Configure properties — Click a node and edit in the inspector
  7. Save — Click "Save Tree" to save to the server

docs.title: Saving
docs.text:
• Save writes the tree to data/skillforge/trees/ on the server
  • Trees are immediately available after saving
  • Players currently viewing the tree will need to re-open to see changes
